instruction: Choose 2 correct options.
questionText: You and your classmates are reading each other's shared digital documents. The document you are reading is well written, but you notice a factual error. You need to respectfully indicate the error to the author in a document comment. Which two actions should you take?

options.label: A
options.text: Provide the correct information in your comment if you have it.

options.label: B
options.text: Point out the error without explanation.

options.label: C
options.text: Write the correction in capital letters to ensure that the author will see it.

options.label: D
options.text: Keep the tone of your correction positive.

instruction: Select Yes or No for each response.
questionText: You find a social media post that shares inaccurate and misleading medical information. You decide to respond to the post. For each response, select Yes if it adheres to appropriate digital etiquette or No if it does not.

options.text: Yes
No

statements.text: "WRONG! Did you do ANY RESEARCH before posting this?"
"Many doctors, including Jane Smith of Harvard Medical School, disagree."
"It can be difficult to research everything, but there are many different sources to look at concerning this issue."
"This is just wrong and makes you look foolish! Obviously you didn't look at the research from Harvard Medical School!"

instruction: Choose 2 correct options.
questionText: You are working on a collaborative team with three other people, and your team is discussing a task over email. Your team disagrees about the task, and you feel strongly that your opinion is correct. Which two steps could you take to deal with this disagreement in a way that follows the standards of digital etiquette?

options.label: A
options.text: Compose an email using strong language that shows how you feel about the task, and send it to everyone in your organization.

options.label: B
options.text: Pause the conversation to research the issue further, then compose a clear email describing your opinion.

options.label: C
options.text: Post about your team's disagreement publicly on social media and ask for the opinions of your friends and followers.

options.label: D
options.text: Schedule an in-person or virtual meeting with the whole team to discuss the task together face-to-face.

instruction: Choose the correct option.
questionText: You need to research whether a snake is a good pet to have. Which search result presents a different perspective from the other three?

options.label: A
options.text: Do Snakes Make Good Pets? — www.snakerights.org — Snakes are wild animals and should never be kept as pets. Even if they are not poisonous, they are dangerous and cannot be tamed.

options.label: B
options.text: How to Choose the Best Pet Snake For You — www.vetmed.famu.edu — Before choosing a pet snake, consider how much space you have to keep your snake. You will need a tank large enough for your snake to grow.

options.label: C
options.text: Snakes as Pets — www.petshop.com — Ball pythons and corn snakes make great pets that are easy to care for, but larger species like boas should be kept only by experienced professionals.

options.label: D
options.text: 11 Best Pet Snakes for Beginners — www.petchronicles.com — One of the best pet snakes for young pet owners is the corn snake. Corn snakes are small, calm, and have a beautiful color pattern.
